one_liner: LULU up 4.9% in confirmed Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B (3/5 signals) with bullish MACD and 7.5% DCF upside — selling a durable premium brand at a discount after a 30%+ drawdown.

news_catalyst.headline: Lululemon Bouncing in Accumulation Phase — No Specific Catalyst Today
news_catalyst.summary: LULU is up 4.9% today with no specific news catalyst — the move appears technical and sentiment-driven as the stock has been oversold. Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B is confirmed with 3/5 signals detected and bullish bias. The stock has pulled back over 30% from highs creating a value entry in a premium athleisure brand with strong brand loyalty. Consumer spending headwinds are real but LULU's premium positioning insulates it more than mass-market apparel.

technical.summary: LULU is in confirmed Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B with 3 of 5 signals detected and bullish structural bias. RSI at 47 is neutral — not yet overbought, with room to run to resistance at $168. MACD in a fresh bullish crossover. Support at $110 is strong. The stock needs a volume expansion day above $120 to confirm Phase C entry.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $126 implies modest 7.5% upside — the negative growth rate (-6.6%) in the model reflects current analyst estimates and keeps the intrinsic value conservative. If LULU can stabilize or grow revenue, the bull case at $148 is more likely.

key_concerns: Negative growth rate in DCF model reflects revenue deceleration
fundamentals.summary: Grade B fundamentals with a brand moat that justifies a premium valuation. The current selldown from $300+ highs reflects growth deceleration, not brand deterioration. A re-acceleration to even modest positive growth would significantly move the DCF.

risks: DCF growth rate is negative (-6.6%) — requires business stabilization to justify thesis
Consumer spending slowdown disproportionately hits discretionary apparel
Resistance at $168 is 43% above current price — a long path to recovery
